loader.HTML:
Choose how to build and follow the steps (~2 min read)

Step 1: Build a Component

Choose a component you know from your product or a side project.

Tell Myop what you want to build or change. Use simple language and add screenshots, mockups, or visual references.

Step 2: Connect to Your App Once

When ready, click Connect.

Myop creates a short snippet to connect the component to your app. This is a one-time setup. Share it with a developer or invite them to your workspace.

Use this option to let Claude read the design directly from a Figma link.

Step 1: Connect Figma MCP to Claude

Step 1: Connect Figma MCP to Claude

Install the Figma MCP connector to give Claude access to your designs and design system.

Step 2: Connect Myop MCP to Claude

Step 2: Connect Myop MCP to Claude

Add the Myop MCP connector so Claude can talk directly to Myop.

Step 3: Build a Component

Step 3: Build a Component

Choose a component you know from your product or a side project.

Add a Figma link and Claude will match your design language automatically. Describe what you want to add or change.

Step 4: Save and Test in Myop

Step 4: Save and Test in Myop

When the component looks right, type: "Create a new component in Myop."

Claude and Myop handle the handoff. In Myop, test different scenarios, validate data flow, and run automated tests as needed.

Step 5: Connect to Your App Once

Step 5: Connect to Your App Once

When ready, click Connect.

Myop creates a short snippet to connect the component to your app. This is a one-time setup. Share it with a developer or invite them to your workspace.

Use this option if you prefer working with screenshots.

Step 1: Connect Myop MCP to Claude

Step 1: Connect Myop MCP to Claude

Add the Myop MCP connector so Claude can talk directly to Myop.

Step 2: Build a Component

Step 2: Build a Component

Choose a component you know, from your product or a side project.

Describe what you want to build or change. Upload screenshots, mockups, or visual references.

Step 3: Save and Test in Myop

Step 3: Save and Test in Myop

When the component looks right, type: "Create a new component in Myop."

Claude and Myop handle the handoff. In Myop, test different scenarios, validate data flow, and run automated tests as needed.

Step 4: Connect to Your App Once

Step 4: Connect to Your App Once

When ready, click Connect.

Myop creates a short snippet to connect the component to your app. This is a one-time setup. Share it with a developer or invite them to your workspace.

Use this option if you're using Gemini or not using Myop MCP.

Step 1: Build in Gemini

Step 1: Build in Gemini

In Gemini, enter this prompt and describe what you want to build. Add screenshots or visual references if possible.

Step 2: Iterate Until Ready

Step 2: Iterate Until Ready

Refine the component in Gemini until it is ready. When finished, copy the generated code.

Step 3: Create the Component in Myop

Step 3: Create the Component in Myop

Open Myop, create a new component, and paste the code.

In some cases, Myop will ask you to update the component with a specific prompt.

Test and preview the component, when done, click Save.

Step 4: Connect to Your App Once

Step 4: Connect to Your App Once

When ready, click Connect.

Myop creates a short snippet to connect the component to your app. This is a one-time setup. Share it with a developer or invite them to your workspace.
